name: Oriental Treasure Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chickens in a large, heavy pot. Add 2 quarts of water, sliced onions, chopped celery, carrots, parsley, salt, and white pepper. Bring to boil.
2. Skim off any scum that rises to the top.
3. Cover and reduce heat. Simmer for 1½ to 2 hours or until chickens are very tender. Remove chickens from the pot and set aside.
4. Strain the broth into a clean container. There should be about 7 cups of broth.
5. Return the broth to the pot and stir in soy sauce, sherry, ginger juice, and sesame oil. Dice the chicken and add it back to the broth. Bring to boil once more.
6. Place the soup in a chafing dish over a heating unit. Keep it at a simmer.
7. Arrange diced tofu, cooked Chinese noodles, sliced green onions, fresh mushrooms, water chestnuts, celery, red peppers, and cilantro leaves in bowls around the chafing dish.
8. Ladle broth into bowls for guests to add their choice of ingredients. Keep the broth very hot as adding extra ingredients will cool it down.
